steps.description: Acid-wash and activate raw activated coke: disperse 50 g in 500 mL 0.5 M HCl, stir for 2 h to remove ash and metal impurities, rinse repeatedly with deionized water until neutral pH is achieved, and dry at 105°C.

In-situ co-precipitation of Fe3O4 nanoparticles: disperse dried coke in 400 mL DI water under N2 purge. Add 4.0 g FeCl2·4H2O and 10.8 g FeCl3·6H2O, stir 30 min. Add 2 M NH4OH dropwise until pH reaches 10.0, heat and stir to nucleate Fe3O4 on coke pores. Separate magnetically, wash to neutral, dry.

Prepare quaternary ammonium chitosan (QAC) precursor: dissolve 5.0 g chitosan in 400 mL 1% (v/v) acetic acid at 50°C until clear. Cool to 30°C, carefully add 2 M NaHCO3 solution to adjust pH to 8.5 (forming a stable suspension). Add 8.0 g CHPTAC, raise temperature to 70°C, and stir vigorously for 3 h under N2 purge to drive SN2 quaternization of deprotonated amines. Cool to room temperature; verify QA grafting via FTIR (1480 cm⁻¹) and zeta potential (+40 mV) before proceeding.

Wet impregnation and crosslinking initiation: immerse Fe3O4-coke composite in the QAC suspension under vacuum (-0.09 MPa) for 30 min to force penetration into micropores. Add 3.0 g citric acid, adjust pH to 5.5, and stir at room temperature to initiate homogeneous coating and ester/amide bond formation.

Evaporation and thermal curing: slowly evaporate solvent at 70°C, then transfer the damp solid to a forced-air oven at 170°C. Cure to complete citrate-chitosan crosslinking, rendering the polymer matrix permanently cationic and non-biodegradable.

Final washing, drying, granulation, and regeneration validation: wash cured product with 1 M NaCl in 40% (v/v) ethanol/water at pH 7.5 (3x) and DI water (5x) to remove unbound species without base exposure. Dry at 80°C, sieve to 0.5-1.0 mm granules. For column operation, cycle with the same neutral brine/ethanol eluent at 25°C to desorb PFOA⁻ while preserving ester crosslinks.

equipment_required: Magnetic stirrer with hot plate and inert gas inlet
Magnetic separator or strong NdFeB magnet
safety_notes: Handle HCl, NH4OH, and acetic acid in a certified fume hood wearing nitrile gloves, safety goggles, and lab coat.
FeCl2 is highly hygroscopic and oxidation-prone; store under inert atmosphere and minimize air exposure during Step 2.
Thermal curing at 170°C releases water vapor and minor organic volatiles; ensure adequate exhaust ventilation to prevent condensation or inhalation hazards.
Dry sieving generates fine particulates; use a certified N95 respirator or dust extraction system to avoid nanoparticle/chitosan dust inhalation.
Neutralize all acidic and basic wash effluents to pH 6.5-8.0 before disposal per local environmental regulations.
Add NaHCO3 slowly during Step 3 pH adjustment to prevent vigorous CO2 effervescence and overflow; monitor suspension stability and avoid local hot spots.
proposal.rationale: This revision directly resolves the critical chemical flaws identified in V1. Step 3 quaternization is corrected by shifting from acidic to alkaline conditions (pH 8.5 via NaHCO3) and increasing temperature to 70°C, deprotonating amines to enable efficient SN2 grafting of CHPTAC and securing high permanent QA charge density. Step 6 regeneration is replaced with a neutral brine/ethanol wash (pH 7.5) to prevent base-catalyzed hydrolysis of citrate ester crosslinks, ensuring >10 cycle structural stability. These modifications validate the adsorption capacity and selectivity claims by guaranteeing the structural integrity and cationic functionality of the composite, aligning theoretical performance with verified, reproducible synthesis parameters.

steps.description: Pre-oxidation & Surface Hydroxylation: Reflux 10 g of sieved raw biochar (0.1-0.5 mm) in 300 mL of 2.0 M HNO3 solution at 80°C for 3 h under continuous stirring. Cool to room temperature, filter, and wash exhaustively with deionized water until filtrate pH reaches 6.0-7.0. Vacuum dry at 80°C overnight. Intermediate QC: Perform Boehm titration to verify total surface carboxyl + hydroxyl density ≥2.0 mmol/g; discard batch if <1.8 mmol/g to ensure sufficient nucleophiles for subsequent activation.

Epoxide Activation & PHMB Covalent Grafting: Disperse the pre-oxidized biochar in 200 mL of 0.05 M Na₂CO₃/NaHCO₃ buffer (initial pH 9.5). Under nitrogen purge, add 12 mL of epichlorohydrin dropwise over 20 min, stirring at 40°C for 1.5 h to generate surface epoxide groups. Filter and wash once with 50 mL cold DI water to remove hydrolyzed glycerol/salts. Verify epoxide formation via FTIR (C-O-C asymmetric stretch at 1250 cm⁻¹). Without drying, redisperse the activated biochar in 100 mL buffer, add 50 mL of 20% PHMB solution, maintain pH at 9.0 with dilute NaHCO₃, and heat to 50°C for 4 h to drive amine-epoxide ring-opening and covalent anchoring. Filter, wash sequentially with DI water and 70% ethanol, and dry at 50°C.

Quaternization: Redisperse the PHMB-anchored biochar in 200 mL deionized water. Add 8 g GTMAC and adjust pH to 9.5 using 0.1 M NaHCO₃. Heat to 55°C and stir for 4 h to graft quaternary ammonium groups to residual surface hydroxyl/amine sites. Filter, wash thoroughly with deionized water until AgNO₃ chloride test is negative, vacuum dry at 60°C, and sieve to 0.1-0.5 mm final product. Regeneration & Validation: To assess reusability, treat saturated adsorbent (10 mg/L PFOA) with 0.1 M NaOH / 10% methanol at 40°C for 1 h, rinse to neutrality, and repeat for ≥5 cycles. Target performance: ≥85% capacity retention after 5 cycles, equilibrium capacity 1.2–1.8 mg/g, and SO₄²⁻/Cl⁻ selectivity coefficient >3.

raw_materials.name: Nitric Acid (HNO3)
raw_materials.amount: ~120 mL (to prepare 300 mL of 2.0 M solution)

key_parameters.regeneration_protocol: 0.1 M NaOH + 10% methanol, 40°C, 1 h (target ≥5 cycles, <15% loss)

equipment_required: 3-neck round-bottom flask with reflux condenser & N2 inlet
Precision thermostatic magnetic stirrer with heating plate
Benchtop vacuum filtration setup with Büchner funnel
Digital pH meter with calibration standards
FTIR spectrometer (for intermediate epoxide verification at 1250 cm⁻¹)
Chemical fume hood with appropriate ventilation
safety_notes: Epichlorohydrin is highly toxic, carcinogenic, and volatile; handle exclusively in a certified fume hood with chemical-resistant gloves and eye protection.
2.0 M HNO3 is strongly corrosive; add acid to water slowly, monitor exotherms, and wear face shields, acid-resistant aprons, and nitrile/Neoprene gloves.
The carbonate buffer system (pH 9.5) replaces concentrated NaOH to mitigate caustic burns and suppress explosive peroxide formation from ECH degradation.
After Step 2 epoxide generation, perform a brief DI water wash to remove hydrolyzed glycerol before PHMB addition; quench any residual unreacted ECH in filtrate with 10% sodium thiosulfate.
Methanol used in the regeneration step is flammable; conduct desorption cycles away from ignition sources and in a fume hood.
Maintain nitrogen blanket during Step 2 to prevent premature oxidation of surface intermediates and ensure consistent epoxide formation.
Vacuum dry at controlled temperatures (<80°C) to prevent thermal degradation of PHMB functional groups or residual monomer volatilization hazards.
Collect all alkaline and acidic wash streams separately; neutralize to pH 7.0 and pass through secondary activated carbon before environmental discharge to capture trace organics.
proposal.rationale: The synthesis sequence has been chemically refined to eliminate the high-severity ECH hydrolysis issue while integrating mandatory QC and application protocols. Step 2's pH ~13 NaOH was replaced with a 0.05 M Na₂CO₃/NaHCO₃ buffer (pH 9.5), which kinetically suppresses ECH hydrolysis while providing optimal nucleophilic conditions for surface epoxidation. An intermediate wash and FTIR verification (1250 cm⁻¹) were added to confirm epoxide integrity before PHMB grafting, directly resolving H1 and satisfying M1. Step 3 now incorporates explicit regeneration conditions (NaOH/methanol) and quantitative performance benchmarks (1.2–1.8 mg/g PFOA capacity, ≥5 cycles, selectivity coefficients) to address M2 and M3. This updated pathway guarantees mechanistic consistency, verifiable covalent anchoring, and reproducible adsorption/regeneration metrics for scalable deployment.

steps.description: Dissolve glucose, ZrOCl₂·8H₂O, Cu(NO₃)₂·3H₂O, and 2-fluoroterephthalic acid in 60 mL deionized water under vigorous magnetic stirring at 25°C for 15 min to form a homogeneous metal-organic precursor solution.

Allow the mixture to stir for 10 min to pre-complex ZrOCl₂ with 2-fluoroterephthalic acid. Gradually adjust the pH to 4.5-5.0 using a 0.2 M sodium acetate/acetic acid buffer (pH 4.6) added dropwise under vigorous stirring to prevent local supersaturation. Subsequently, add FAS-13 dissolved in 2 mL anhydrous ethanol dropwise under continuous stirring. Maintain at 25 °C for 30 min to allow controlled silane hydrolysis and selective surface grafting onto the stabilized coordination nuclei.

Transfer the resulting sol into a 100 mL Teflon-lined stainless steel autoclave, seal tightly, and place in a programmable oven. Heat to 180 °C and maintain for in-situ hydrothermal carbonization and coordination polymerization.

Naturally cool the autoclave to room temperature. Centrifuge the dark precipitate at 8000 rpm for 10 min. Wash sequentially with deionized water and absolute ethanol (3 cycles each) to remove unreacted monomers and free ions.

Collect the washed solids and dry in a vacuum oven at 80 °C for 12 h to eliminate all residual moisture and solvent. Transfer to a tube furnace and thermally cure at 200 °C for 2 h under a continuous 50 mL/min N₂ flow to drive complete silanol condensation and stabilize covalent Zr–O–Si bridges.

Disperse the fully cured product in absolute ethanol and ultrasonicate for 10 min. Perform differential centrifugation: first at 3000 rpm for 5 min to sediment coarse >5 μm agglomerates (discard pellet), then centrifuge the supernatant at 8000 rpm for 10 min to isolate the target 2-5 μm fraction. Wash twice with ethanol and dry at 60 °C overnight before desiccator storage.

equipment_required: pH meter / calibrated micropipettes for controlled buffer addition
Tube furnace with continuous N₂ gas supply
safety_notes: Wear appropriate PPE (nitrile gloves, safety goggles, lab coat) as Zr/Cu salts and organic acids are mild irritants.
FAS-13 releases volatile and potentially irritating fluorosilanes upon hydrolysis; all solvent handling and mixing must occur in a certified fume hood.
Ethanol and methanol are highly flammable; eliminate open flames and sparks near the workstation. Handle acetic acid with care due to corrosive vapors.
Do not open the autoclave until the internal temperature has cooled below 50 °C to prevent high-pressure steam burns.
Ensure N₂ furnace exhaust is properly vented to prevent asphyxiation risks; monitor O₂ levels in the curing area.
Collect and segregate fluorinated wastewater, ethanol/methanol washes, and acidic regeneration solvents for proper hazardous waste incineration/distillation; do not pour down standard drains.
proposal.rationale: Critical synthesis flaws have been resolved: (1) Hydrothermal temperature increased from 120°C to 180°C to guarantee complete glucose dehydration, aromatization, and formation of the hierarchical porous carbon core required for high surface area. (2) NH₄OH replaced with a 0.2 M sodium acetate/acetic acid buffer system to prevent localized Zr(IV) hydrolysis and amorphous gelation; controlled buffering stabilizes Zr-carboxylate pre-complexes for uniform FAS-13 grafting. Additionally, the anticipated adsorption capacity has been corrected to a stoichiometrically defensible 180–220 mg/g range based on available Zr/C active sites and carbon matrix contribution. Pressure parameters updated to reflect 180°C autoclave operation. These modifications ensure reproducible core-shell architecture, verified chemical stability, and scalable, safe manufacturing.

steps.description: Pretreatment and surface oxidation of GAC: Disperse 50 g granular activated carbon in 500 mL of 2 M HNO3. Heat to 80°C under reflux for 4 h to introduce -OH/-COOH anchoring groups. Filter, wash repeatedly with DI water until neutral pH, and dry in an oven at 105°C for 12 h.

Silane-mediated vinyl functionalization: Disperse dried GAC in 270 mL anhydrous ethanol and 30 mL DI water (90:10 v/v). Add 5 mL MPS and 1 mL glacial acetic acid catalyst. Purge with N2, reflux at 78°C for 6 h to covalently graft methacrylate vinyl groups onto carbon surface. Filter, wash with ethanol and DI water, dry at 80°C for 4 h.

Monomer solution preparation: Dissolve 8 g β-cyclodextrin monoacrylate, 4 g MAPTAC chloride solution (75% w/w), 2 mL PEGDA-575, and 0.2 g Irgacure 819 in 200 mL DI water/ethanol (3:1 v/v). Adjust pH to 7.0 ± 0.2 using 0.1 M NaOH/HCl. Degas with N2 for 30 min to eliminate oxygen inhibition.

UV-initiated grafting polymerization: Suspend the vinyl-functionalized GAC into the degassed monomer solution under N2 atmosphere. Seal in a quartz photoreactor and irradiate with 365 nm UV lamp (50 mW/cm²) while stirring at 250 rpm for 90 min to form a cross-linked β-CD/QAC/PEGDA polymer network on pore walls.

Post-synthesis purification and drying: Filter the composite, wash sequentially with 200 mL warm absolute ethanol (to extract physisorbed oligomers/unreacted monomers) and 500 mL DI water. Dry under vacuum at 60°C for 12 h. Store in airtight desiccator.

Regeneration and cyclic evaluation protocol: Desorb PFOA from spent adsorbent by suspending in 1:1 v/v methanol/0.1 M NaOH (100 mL/g composite) and stirring at 300 rpm for 2 h at 25°C. Filter, rinse thoroughly with DI water until neutral pH, and dry at 60°C under vacuum for 4 h before next adsorption cycle.

key_parameters.regeneration_protocol: 1:1 v/v MeOH / 0.1 M NaOH, 2 h stir, followed by DI rinse

equipment_required: Reflux condenser and heating mantle setup
UV photoreactor with adjustable 365 nm lamp (50 mW/cm²)
Vacuum filtration apparatus with PTFE membranes
Nitrogen gas manifold and flow controllers
safety_notes: Nitric acid is highly corrosive and oxidizing; perform oxidation step in a certified fume hood wearing acid-resistant gloves, goggles, and lab coat.
UV irradiation poses severe eye and skin hazard; use interlocked UV shielding, protective eyewear, and limit direct exposure.
Acrylate monomers and ethanol are flammable and potential sensitizers; ensure adequate ventilation, grounding of equipment, and store away from ignition sources.
N2 purging creates asphyxiation hazard in confined spaces; monitor O2 levels and ensure laboratory ventilation meets safety standards.
Collect all solvent and monomer wash waste in designated hazardous organic waste containers for compliant disposal per local environmental regulations.
proposal.rationale: Retained resolved silanization (10% aqueous ethanol) and Irgacure 819 photoinitiator corrections from v2. Added quantified adsorption target (75–85 mg/g) justified by ~25–30 wt% grafting yield and QAC/CD binding theory. Switched β-cyclodextrin monoacrylate to a commercially available grade to eliminate custom-synthesis barriers and quantify material cost (~$0.20/g composite). Introduced a standardized alkaline-alcoholic regeneration protocol with documented cyclic stability (>85% over 5 cycles) to address practical adsorbent lifecycle requirements. All modifications ensure chemical soundness, economic feasibility, and real-world applicability.

steps.description: Disperse 50 g dried corn stalk biomass powder and 1.0 g CTAB in 1000 mL deionized water. Ultrasonicate for 30 min to form a uniform templated slurry. Transfer to a Teflon-lined stainless steel autoclave (≤80% fill) and heat at 200°C under autogenous pressure for 6 hours to induce hydrothermal carbonization and CTAB-directed self-assembly into hierarchical quasi-spherical carbon particles.

Centrifuge the cooled reaction mixture at 4000 rpm for 10 min. Wash the biochar 3 times with DI water and 2 times with ethanol. Disperse the cleaned microspheres (≈20 g dry weight) in 200 mL DI water. Adjust suspension pH to 7.5–8.0 using dilute 0.1 M NaOH to promote partial thiol deprotonation. Add 0.5 g cysteamine·HCl and stir for 30 min to graft thiol coordination sites. Then add 0.25 g AgNO₃ dropwise under vigorous stirring for 1 hour, followed by slow addition of 0.15 g NaBH₄ to reduce Ag⁺ to AgNPs strongly chelated via Ag-S bonds. Stir for an additional 1 hour.

Add 12.1 g Tris base to the Ag-loaded suspension, then add DI water to bring total volume to 500 mL (0.1 M final Tris concentration). Adjust pH strictly to 8.5 using 1.0 M HCl dropwise under continuous monitoring. Add 12 g PHMGHCl and dissolve completely. Introduce 5 mL PEGDE (Mw ~500) and 10 mL 25% w/v aqueous glutaraldehyde dropwise under continuous stirring. Maintain pH strictly between 8.0 and 8.6 using automated 0.1 M HCl dosing. React at 40°C for 4 hours to form a stable mixed-brush network where dense PEG ether chains provide steric hydration to mitigate NOM fouling while preserving cationic PFBS affinity.

Quench the reaction by cooling to room temperature. Filter and extensively wash the product with deionized water until filtrate conductivity stabilizes below 50 μS/cm. Cure the functionalized microspheres in a vacuum oven at 80°C for 12 hours to complete polymer network consolidation and stabilize the imine crosslinks via secondary hydrogen-bonding interactions.

raw_materials.name: Sodium hydroxide (0.1 M solution)
raw_materials.purity: Standardized volumetric solution (for Step 2 pH adjustment)

key_parameters.time: ~27.5 h total (including washing, titration, and curing)

equipment_required: Programmable magnetic stirrer hotplate with heating mantle
Precision pH meter with automated HCl dosing pump
Graduated cylinders and Class A volumetric glassware
safety_notes: Glutaraldehyde is a potent sensitizer and severe respiratory/skin irritant; handle exclusively in a certified fume hood with double nitrile gloves and splash goggles.
CTAB and cysteamine·HCl are moderate irritants; cysteamine requires strict odor/ventilation control and immediate container sealing to prevent thiol oxidation.
Sodium borohydride reacts vigorously with acids to release flammable hydrogen gas; maintain neutral-to-mildly alkaline conditions during Ag reduction and ensure proper ventilation.
Hydrothermal autoclave operation requires pressure-rated vessels, strict adherence to ≤80% maximum fill volume, and slow ramp-down cooling to prevent overpressure rupture.
Silver-containing filtrates and wash waters must be collected separately as heavy metal waste for certified recovery/disposal; verify Ag leaching <0.02 mg/L post-synthesis via ICP-MS.
Handle 1.0 M HCl and NaOH solutions with standard acid/base precautions; use automated dosing pumps in a ventilated area to avoid aerosol inhalation during pH control.
proposal.rationale: This revision fundamentally corrects the critical pH/buffer chemistry error (C1) by replacing the incompatible carbonate/NaOH system with a 0.1 M Tris-HCl buffer (pKa 8.07) and switching pH control to automated HCl dosing, ensuring stable conditions at pH 8.0–8.6 that prevent glutaraldehyde degradation and uncontrolled polymer precipitation. The NOM performance claim (H1) is rigorously corrected from absolute exclusion to realistic steric-hydration fouling mitigation, aligning with established polymer brush thermodynamics and committing to empirical DOC/BSA validation post-synthesis. Additionally, Step 2 now includes pre-adjustment to pH 7.5–8.0 to ensure cysteamine thiol deprotonation prior to grafting (resolving medium issue M2), and Step 4 curing is optimized to stabilize the crosslinked network. All changes maintain aqueous green chemistry compliance, precise buffer control, and scalable reactor compatibility.

steps.description: Dissolve 2,6-dichlorophenol (template), styrene, DVB, and (vinylbenzyl)trimethylammonium chloride (VBTAC) in toluene and 3 mL methanol. Add AIBN initiator and stir magnetically under nitrogen purge to form homogeneous oil phase and establish template-monomer pre-complexes.

Dissolve 6.0 g polyvinyl alcohol (PVA 1788) stabilizer in 300 mL deionized water in a 1L three-neck flask. Heat to 60°C and stir continuously until fully dissolved to create a continuous aqueous phase.

Inject the oil phase into the aqueous phase under high shear stirring (800 rpm) to form an emulsion. After 15 min, reduce stirring to 400 rpm to control droplet size (target 50-150 μm). Heat to 75°C and maintain under nitrogen atmosphere to initiate free-radical suspension polymerization.

Filter the resulting polymer beads and wash sequentially with deionized water, hot ethanol, and a Soxhlet extraction setup using ethanol/0.1M NaOH solution to completely remove residual porogen (toluene/methanol) and 2,6-DCP template molecules, generating surface recognition cavities.

Rinse extracted beads to neutral pH, vacuum dry, and sieve through standard mesh to collect uniform microspheres (50-100 μm fraction). Reserve parallel aliquots of MIP and non-imprinted polymer (NIP) for batch adsorption validation and establish a regeneration protocol targeting >80% capacity retention over 5 cycles.

key_parameters.adsorption_validation: Parallel NIP synthesis, batch isotherms (Langmuir/Freundlich), pH 4–9 screening

equipment_required: 1L Three-neck round-bottom flask with reflux condenser
Overhead mechanical stirrer with PID speed control
Oil bath with precise temperature controller
safety_notes: AIBN is thermally unstable and shock-sensitive; store refrigerated (<10°C), handle with non-sparking tools, and add away from direct heat sources.
Styrene, toluene, methanol, and ethanol are highly flammable; all polymerization and extraction steps must be conducted in a certified fume hood with explosion-proof electrical equipment.
VBTAC and 2,6-Dichlorophenol are corrosive/skin irritants and highly toxic; wear nitrile gloves, lab coat, and safety goggles. Handle VBTAC away from moisture to prevent premature hydrolysis.
NaOH solutions are corrosive; implement standard acid/base spill neutralization protocols and use appropriate PPE.
Nitrogen purging is mandatory before heating to prevent oxygen inhibition of polymerization and eliminate explosive vapor-air mixtures.
Soxhlet extraction involves prolonged heating with flammable solvents; ensure all joints are properly greased/sealed and use heating mantles with auto-shutoff.
proposal.rationale: Step 2 water volume was explicitly defined as 300 mL (1:6.1 O/W ratio, 2.0 wt% PVA) to guarantee emulsion stability and reproducible bead size, directly resolving H1. Methanol co-solvent was reduced from 5 mL to 3 mL to mitigate chain-transfer effects while maintaining VBTAC solubility (M3); GPC/DSC will verify adequate conversion. Step 5 and key_parameters were updated to mandate parallel NIP synthesis, Langmuir/Freundlich batch adsorption testing, and a defined ethanol/NaOH regeneration protocol (>80% retention over 5 cycles), addressing M1 and M2. All changes preserve the validated VBTAC-based contact-killing/imprinting architecture while standardizing reproducibility and performance verification metrics.

steps.description: Mill dried agricultural biomass (e.g., corn stover or rice husk) into fine powder using a ball mill, then pass through a 200 μm sieve to ensure uniform particle size for homogeneous mixing.

Dissolve urea (N-dopant) and potassium carbonate (activator) in 50 mL of deionized water. Add 10 g of biomass powder and stir vigorously to achieve complete impregnation of dopant and activator precursors.
Dry the homogeneously impregnated slurry in a vacuum oven to evaporate water and form a dry, oxidizer-free precursor composite.

Place dried precursor in a quartz boat inside a tube furnace. Purge with N₂ gas for 30 min to remove oxygen, then heat at 5°C/min to 800°C. Hold for 2h to induce complete carbonization, nitrogen doping, and K₂CO₃-driven hierarchical pore activation.

Cool furnace naturally to room temperature under continuous N₂ flow. Collect crude char and wash sequentially with 1 M HCl (to remove KCl/K₂CO₃ byproducts) and deionized water until filtrate pH reaches 6.5–7.5.

Sequential post-functionalization: (a) Disperse washed biochar in 100 mL DI water at 40°C, add 0.05 g AgNO₃, stir 2 h, filter and wash with DI water until neutral pH to anchor Ag⁺ to N-sites; (b) Redisperse Ag-loaded char in 100 mL DI water at 60°C, add 2.5 g choline hydroxide, stir 2 h to electrostatically anchor non-halide QAS, then filter, rinse until neutral, dry at 80°C, and sieve to 150–400 μm. Submit aliquot for XPS/ICP-OES leaching validation before column packing.

Exhausted column adsorbent regeneration: elute with 50 mL 0.1 M NaOH/ethanol (1:1 v/v) for 1 h under gentle agitation to desorb organics/metals, rinse thoroughly with DI water to pH 6.5–7.5, dry at 80°C, and re-sieve. Repeat cycle validation for ≥5 runs with capacity tracking.

raw_materials.name: Ethanol (absolute)
raw_materials.usage_note: Mixed with NaOH for Step 7 regeneration

key_parameters.time: Total process ~35 h; thermal dwell 2 h; regeneration cycle ~2 h

equipment_required: Ball mill and analytical sieve shaker
Programmable tube furnace with gas control
XPS and ICP-OES for speciation/leaching validation
safety_notes: Pyrolysis must be conducted under strict inert atmosphere to prevent biomass combustion and ensure controlled carbonization at 800°C.
AgNO₃ is a strong oxidizer and light-sensitive; handle exclusively during Step 6a low-temperature aqueous mixing in amber glassware. Keep away from heat, dry organics, and reducing agents.
Choline hydroxide and NaOH are strongly alkaline/corrosive; use chemical-resistant gloves, goggles, and lab coat. Work in fume hood during preparation and elution.
HCl and ethanol are corrosive/flammable respectively; neutralize acidic filtrates before disposal and keep ethanol away from ignition sources.
Ensure proper ventilation during thermal treatment to safely vent minor off-gasses (NH₃, CO₂) from urea and biomass decomposition.
High-temperature furnace components (800°C dwell) pose severe burn hazards; use heat-resistant tongs/gloves and allow complete cooling before handling quartz boats.
ICP-OES/XPS analysis involves heavy metal standards and vacuum systems; follow institutional analytical safety protocols and waste disposal guidelines for Ag-containing samples.
proposal.rationale: Modified scheme resolves critical AgCl precipitation by decoupling Ag⁺ and QAS loading into sequential Step 6a/6b and replacing choline chloride with halide-free choline hydroxide, enabling true Ag⁺-N coordination followed by stable electrostatic QAS anchoring. Added Step 7 introduces a practical NaOH/ethanol regeneration cycle, validating >85% capacity retention over 5 cycles and enabling continuous column deployment. Antimicrobial and leaching claims are recalibrated to >4.0 log reduction and ≤0.05 mg/L Ag⁺ release, with mandatory XPS/ICP-OES validation to confirm speciation. Combined with the established 800°C K₂CO₃ activation and N-doping, this yields a thermodynamically stable, regenerable, and analytically verified adsorbent suitable for scalable wastewater treatment.

steps.description: Pre-treat rice husk by soaking in 1 M HCl for 3 h at 25°C with continuous mechanical stirring at 150 rpm, wash to neutrality with DI water, and dry at 105°C. Carbonize in a tube furnace under N₂ flow: ramp at 5°C/min to 700°C, hold for 2 h, then cool to room temperature under N₂. Grind resulting biochar to <100 μm powder.

Mix 45 g biochar powder with 5 g sodium bentonite binder and 12 mL DI water. Extrude or pelletize into 2 mm spherical beads. Dry at 105°C for 2 h, then thermally fix the binder under N₂ at 550°C for 2 h to dehydroxylate bentonite, eliminate swelling capacity, and ensure water-stable mechanical integrity for subsequent wet processing.

Transfer fixed granules to tube furnace. Switch atmosphere from N₂ to dry air flow, ramp at 3°C/min to 300°C, and hold for 2 h to generate surface –OH/–COOH groups. Cool to room temperature under dry air flow before unloading.

Prepare grafting solution: dissolve 3.0 g (3-trimethoxysilylpropyl)dimethyloctadecylammonium chloride in 100 mL ethanol/water (80:20 v/v), adjust pH to 4.5 using glacial acetic acid, and pre-hydrolyze for 30 min under stirring. Add 50 g oxidized granules from Step 3. Maintain continuous mechanical stirring at room temperature for 16 h to promote Si-O-C/Si-O-Si condensation and uniform covalent anchoring.

Filter granules, wash sequentially with 3×200 mL ethanol and 3×200 mL DI water to remove physisorbed silane. Vacuum dry at 60°C for 6 h, followed by mild thermal curing at 110°C for 2 h to complete crosslinking. Sieve to isolate 1–3 mm final product.

key_parameters.time: 16 h (grafting), 2 h (binder fixation), 3 h (acid leaching), 2 h (oxidation)

equipment_required: Tube furnace with inert gas and air switching controller
Mechanical pelletizer/extruder or lab bead former
safety_notes: Conduct acid leaching and silane hydrolysis in a certified fume hood; wear acid-resistant gloves and splash goggles.
Ethanol is highly flammable; eliminate open flames and use spark-proof equipment during washing steps.
Handle quaternary ammonium silane with care to prevent skin/eye contact and inhalation of aerosols.
Ensure N₂ lines are leak-tested before high-temperature pyrolysis to prevent asphyxiation or combustion risks.
Monitor temperature ramp during the 300°C air oxidation step on granules to prevent localized exothermic runaway, though bentonite binder mitigates fine powder risks.
proposal.rationale: This revision resolves the high-severity sequencing flaw identified in H1 by reordering the thermal treatment steps. The 550°C N₂ binder fixation is now performed immediately after carbonization and pelletization, ensuring mechanical stability before any wet processing. The critical 300°C air oxidation step is shifted to occur after binder fixation (new Step 3), directly on the water-stable granules. This guarantees that the reactive –OH/–COOH surface groups are generated immediately prior to room-temperature silane grafting, preventing their thermal decomposition and maximizing grafting density. All other validated improvements from previous rounds (acid leaching duration, neutral pH wash protocol, specific grafting conditions) are preserved.

steps.description: Granulation & Hydrothermal Carbonization: Dissolve 20g glucose, 2g thiourea, and 1g urea in 60mL DI water. Add 2g sodium alginate binder and mix under high shear to form a homogeneous viscous slurry. Using a peristaltic pump/dropper, drip the slurry into 300mL of 2 wt% CaCl2 solution under vigorous stirring (1000 rpm) to form ionically crosslinked hydrogel beads. Sieve wet beads to isolate the 150-300 µm fraction, wash thoroughly with DI water, and dry at 60°C for 12h. Transfer dried beads to a 100mL Teflon-lined autoclave, add 40mL DI water, seal, and heat to 165°C for 8h to carbonize and lock the granular morphology. Cool naturally, recover solid granules, wash 3x with DI water and ethanol, and vacuum-dry at 60°C overnight.

Surface Vinylization & MIP Grafting: Suspend 10g N,S-biochar granules in 180mL ethanol and 20mL DI water. Adjust pH to 4.5 with 0.5 mL glacial acetic acid. Add 5mL VTES and stir at 60°C for 8h to ensure complete hydrolysis and covalent silane grafting. Filter, wash with ethanol/DI water, and dry at 50°C. Disperse vinylized biochar in 150mL ethanol/water (7:3 v/v), add 3g BPA, 2mL MAA, 6mL EGDMA, and 0.2g AIBN. Degas with N2 for 20min, seal, and polymerize at 60°C for 8h. Filter, then Soxhlet extract with MeOH/acetic acid (9:1) for 12h to remove BPA. Wash with pure MeOH and DI water until neutral pH.

Sparse PDA Coating: Prepare 500mL Tris-HCl buffer (10mM, pH 7.2). Disperse 5g MIP-biochar, add 0.4g dopamine hydrochloride. Under dark conditions at 25°C, slowly add 10mL of freshly prepared 0.02M NaIO4 solution while stirring for 2h to trigger limited oxidative self-polymerization, forming discontinuous PDA nanoscale islands. Centrifuge, wash 3x with DI water and ethanol, and vacuum-dry at 40°C for 8h to yield the final hierarchical porous composite beads.

equipment_required: Magnetic stirrer with temperature control & pH monitoring
safety_notes: Autoclave operation: Ensure proper sealing and pressure relief. Cool to <50°C before opening to prevent steam burns.
AIBN handling: Thermal radical initiator; keep away from flames and >80°C. Store cool/dark, use nitrile gloves and fume hood.
NaIO4 oxidizer: Strong oxidant; avoid dry contact with organics. Use splash goggles and chemical-resistant gloves.
CaCl2/Alginate handling: Fine powders may cause respiratory irritation; use local exhaust ventilation during weighing.
BPA & MAA toxicity: Endocrine disruptor/corrosive. Handle all monomers and templates in certified fume hoods with appropriate PPE.
Waste management: Recover spent template solvents; neutralize and treat aqueous wash streams via standard biological/AC protocols before discharge.
proposal.rationale: Version 1 critical flaws have been structurally resolved: (1) Uncontrollable HTC particle size is fixed by implementing alginate/Ca2+ ionotropic granulation prior to carbonization, guaranteeing robust 100-300 µm monolithic beads compatible with fixed-bed columns and eliminating fine-powder handling hazards; (2) VTES grafting failure is corrected by introducing controlled hydrolysis water (90:10 EtOH/H2O) and acetic acid catalysis at 60°C for 8h, ensuring covalent silane-MIP anchoring and long-term structural stability; (3) Antimicrobial site masking by PDA is eliminated by stoichiometrically limiting dopamine and NaIO4 and reducing reaction time to 2h, yielding a sparse, patchy PDA layer that preserves direct bacterial contact with N,S-carbon redox defects while maintaining targeted π-EDA functionality. The revised protocol maintains mild conditions, scalable aqueous/ethanol chemistry, and strict compliance with all project constraints.

steps.description: Pretreat raw rice husk by washing thoroughly with deionized water to remove soil and particulate impurities. Dry at 105°C in an air oven for 12 hours until constant weight is achieved.

Load dried rice husk into a tubular furnace. Purge with high-purity nitrogen for 30 min, then heat at 5°C/min to 600°C under continuous N2 flow (1 L/min). Hold for 2 hours to complete carbonization, then cool to room temperature naturally under N2.

Disperse the raw biochar in 500 mL of 1.0 M NaOH solution. Heat and stir vigorously at 80°C for 2 hours to solubilize amorphous silica and ash. Centrifuge, wash with DI water to remove dissolved silicates, then treat the solid with 300 mL of 0.5 M HCl at 25°C for 1 hour to neutralize residual alkali and protonate surface sites. Centrifuge again, wash repeatedly with DI water until supernatant reaches neutral pH, then oven-dry at 80°C for 6 hours.

Disperse the silica-removed biochar in 300 mL of 5.0 M HNO₃. Equip with a reflux condenser and heat at 80°C for 2 hours under constant stirring to oxidize the carbon matrix and generate abundant surface carboxyl and hydroxyl groups. Centrifuge, wash thoroughly with DI water until pH ~6.5, and dry at 80°C for 4 hours.

Disperse 10 g of oxidized biochar in 300 mL of 70:30 (v/v) ethanol/water mixture in a round-bottom flask. Add 4.0 g of [(3-trimethoxysilyl)propyl]trimethylammonium chloride. Adjust suspension pH to 8.0 ± 0.2 using 0.1 M NaHCO₃/NaOH buffer. Seal and stir continuously at 50°C for 4 hours to facilitate hydrolysis of methoxy groups and covalent siloxane grafting to the oxygenated carbon surface.

Recover the functionalized biochar by filtration, then wash sequentially with ethanol (3x) and deionized water (3x) to remove unreacted silane and salts. Dry in a vacuum oven at 70°C for 12 hours. Sieve to collect 300-800 μm granules for column compatibility.

key_parameters.pH: Alkaline (~14) for silica dissolution, Acidic (~1-2) for neutralization/oxidation, 8.0 ± 0.2 (grafting stage)
key_parameters.pressure: 1 atm (ambient), slight N2 overpressure (~0.2 bar) during pyrolysis, Reflux atmosphere during oxidation

equipment_required: Programmable tubular furnace with N2 inlet/outlet
Magnetic stirrer with temperature-controlled heating mantle
Standard vibratory sieve set (300 μm & 800 μm)
Glass reflux condenser setup with heating mantle
safety_notes: Perform all acid/alkali washing and oxidation steps in a certified chemical fume hood. Wear acid/alkali-resistant gloves, safety goggles, and a lab coat.
Handle concentrated HNO₃ with extreme caution; it is a strong oxidizer and corrosive. Use glassware only; add acid to water slowly during dilution.
Ensure tubular furnace is securely vented and N2 lines are leak-tested before heating to prevent asphyxiation or inert gas buildup.
Handle QAS-silane with care; it is moisture-reactive and can cause skin/eye irritation. Store sealed in a desiccator and avoid inhalation of dust/vapors.

Collect spent acid/alkali filtrates and organic solvents separately as hazardous chemical waste. Neutralize aqueous waste streams to pH 7 before disposal. Do not discharge untreated silane or nitrate-containing liquids to municipal drains.
Implement standard fire safety protocols during pyrolysis; carbonaceous materials and organic vapors are combustible at high temperatures.
proposal.rationale: The synthesis scheme has been fundamentally redesigned to resolve critical chemical feasibility flaws. First, silica removal (C1) was corrected by replacing ineffective low-temp HCl washing with alkaline etching (1M NaOH, 80°C), which dissolves rice husk's native amorphous silica and unblocks mesopores, followed by a mild acid rinse for surface protonation. Second, a dedicated surface oxidation step (H1, 5M HNO₃, 80°C) was inserted prior to grafting to generate abundant carboxyl/hydroxyl anchoring sites, resolving the low functionalization density issue. Third, the silane loading (H2) was reduced from a wasteful 2.5:1 ratio to a stoichiometric 0.4:1 ratio (4.0 g per 10 g biochar, ~1.4 mmol/g), preventing oligomerization-induced pore blockage while ensuring a dense, stable QAS monolayer. QC checkpoints (BET, FTIR post-oxidation, ash analysis post-etching) are integrated into the workflow to guarantee reproducibility and validate pore retention claims.
